data.content:
I can see you have an MSTest project targeting .NET 8.0. The express test expects `15.00` but the code returns `10.00`, so that one will fail.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically when you save changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest project with .NET 8.0. They want hot reload to iterate on failing tests. I can set up `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests automatically when files change.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` for them. First, let me run the tests to see the current state, then set up the watch.

Actually, let me just start `dotnet watch test` directly in async mode so they can iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-cd3d3357ef094f2eb185ef06e01b5085 && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test with hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "dotnet-watch"}
